description_long: This is a tall deciduous tree with a straight, sturdy trunk and a moderately dense canopy of green foliage. The trunk is relatively slender at the base, with a rough, textured bark that appears grayish-brown. The branches extend outward and upward in an alternate pattern, supporting clusters of broad, simple leaves. The canopy is somewhat irregular, allowing some light to pass through, and the foliage is concentrated toward the upper half of the tree. No roots are visible above ground, and the tree's overall form is typical of those found in urban parks, streets, or residential neighborhoods.
description: A tall deciduous tree with a straight trunk, rough bark, and a moderately dense canopy of green broad leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the tree, with the trunk clearly visible and the canopy spreading symmetrically.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the irregular, natural spread of branches and the density of the foliage.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear view of the trunk and the alternate branching pattern, showing some gaps in the canopy.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with the canopy appearing slightly denser on the left side.
